name: Kristoffer Dominic Amora
title: Engineering Leader
thesis: Building at the hardware–software seam for 21 years.
summary: Engineering leader with 21 years of experience, including 8+ years managing teams across healthcare, wellness-tech, and retail. Patent-holding inventor with experience in cloud, web, and embedded systems, delivering scalable solutions from startups to Fortune 10 companies, and recent hands-on training in LLM-based agentic systems and responsible Generative AI.

competencies.name: Tech + people leadership
competencies.detail: Engineering team leadership and agile delivery; people leadership; recruitment and engineering team building at scale

competencies.name: Innovation and R&D
competencies.detail: Digital health, wellness tech, retail innovation; patent-holding inventor with hands-on R&D experience

competencies.name: Software, web, embedded
competencies.detail: Scalable software & systems architecture (cloud to edge); embedded systems, BLE/LoRa, and firmware development

skills.items: Agile Scrum, Kanban, Rally, Atlassian JIRA, Miro, MermaidJS, Parabol, Linear
.NET Core, C#, Python, Ionic, PWA, TypeScript, Angular, Node.js, Sauce Labs, Datadog, Docker, Cypress, CI/CD, Snyk, AWS, Azure, Git, GitHub
Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG), LangGraph, LangChain, LLM tools, ReAct agentic patterns, Responsible AI
C, C++, Python, Bluetooth LE, LoRa, LoRaWAN, AcSIP S76S SoM, nRF52xxx, Zephyr RTOS, Nordic Semiconductor, Microchip, Qualcomm, Quectel

bullets: Led a 7-member backend engineering team for UnitedHealthcare Motion, a wellness incentive platform by UnitedHealth Group (#3 on the 2025 Fortune 500), enabling 800K+ members to earn up to $1,000 annually through wearable-tracked fitness goals.
Delivered key features including an employer-facing dashboard and multi-factor authentication (MFA) hardening.
Supported the transition and decommissioning of UHC Motion after a decade of service — seamless member migration and backend integration into the new UHC Rewards platform.
Currently lead a QA-focused team managing automated and manual regression testing for the UHC Rewards module of the UHC mobile app — ranked #4 in the App Store's Medical category.
Pioneered a technical recruitment strategy: 50+ engineers hired in ~10 months to support rapid team expansion in Cebu, Philippines.
From Business Systems Analyst to IT Manager, leading a software team delivering digital health solutions for patient-centered care.
Drove the IT division's Agile transformation: Scrum, Kanban, Git, DevOps, automated E2E tests, and security vulnerability scanning across legacy healthcare systems.

Designed and launched DOKi, a cross-platform PWA adopted by 700+ doctors — real-time patient data, appointments, SMS notifications, and digital professional-fee settlement. 100% adoption in under 9 months.
Delivered executive dashboards: hospital census, admissions, length of stay, ER activity, searchable case rates by ICD codes.
Promoted a UX-first culture with design systems and user-centric development for healthcare professionals and patients.
Co-founded a startup advancing IoT and Industry 4.0 in the Philippines.
Built low-power remote monitoring prototypes on Semtech LoRa and Bluetooth LE over the Nordic nRF stack — similar to what later gained traction as Meshtastic.
Early-stage startup focused on warehouse automation: AngularJS front-end with MySQL backend for 8 clients, migrating material-handling legacy systems to a modular, portable platform.

experience.role: Sr. Firmware / Software Developer, Far East Advance Development Group
bullets: Led firmware development of TMX Philippines' first locally developed digital fitness wristband prototype.
Explored OEM opportunities in the healthcare wellness sector with a cross-functional team.

experience.role: Sr. Device Firmware Developer, Rapid R&D
bullets: Led R&D of firmware and wireless embedded devices pairing with fitness trackers; onsite engineering with contract manufacturers in Shenzhen, China.
Shipped 75K+ hardware units developed and manufactured in the Philippines.
Helped build the engineering team; launched the company's first fitness activity tracker within a year.
Contributed to the formation of Fortify Technologies as a new business unit.
Led development of low-level software tools for NCR barcode scanners, cutting customer reliance on technical support by 70%+.
Scaled the team 200% (8 → 25) in under 2 years as Team Lead — one of NCR Cebu's top-performing teams.
Led a research sub-team on 5+ year horizon technologies in retail, banking, and hospitality, modeled after Google Labs.
2 granted US patents plus internal invention disclosures and research papers in HCI, computer vision, and embedded systems.
Prototyped a vision-based product identification system reducing retail shrinkage and barcode fraud by ~85%.

patents.title: Produce and Non-produce Verification Using Hybrid Scanner
patents.note: Granted May 2020. Verifying produce with a low-resolution image scanner by extracting visual features — lines, corners, text, texture, edges — and comparing against known profiles.

patents.title: Methods to Improve Automated Check-in
patents.note: Granted Jan 2014. Automated check-in via mobile device: user data is encoded into a scannable code (e.g. QR), read at the check-in location, and transmitted to its database.

projects.title: Hospital Digital Transformation & the DOKi App
projects.note: An app offering doctors a new way to view admitted patients, appointments, and notifications, with seamless online professional-fee submission.

projects.title: Bluetooth Smart USB Dongle
projects.note: BLE + USB HID data bridge between wearables and PCs. 75K+ pieces mass-produced in Laguna (PCBA) and Cebu (final assembly + mass firmware flashing).

projects.title: Machine Code to Human-readable Interface
projects.note: NCR RealScan EEPROM Configuration Tool — 800+ UI components generated at runtime from XML, before XAML made that normal.

awards.title: 2nd Runner-up — Digital Technology for Charities DeepHack Hackathon
Accepted — MIT–Harvard Medical School Healthcare Innovation Bootcamp

education.school: University of San Carlos — Technological Center
